steps.action: Sign in to your cloud provider's management console with a read-only role
steps.security_note: Never use root/admin credentials for routine evidence collection

steps.action: Navigate to the audit log or activity log section of the console
steps.audit_note: Most cloud providers retain activity logs for 90 days by default

steps.action: Filter the log by date range, event type, and any relevant resource filters
steps.audit_note: Document your filter criteria for reproducibility

steps.action: Export the filtered results as CSV or JSON
steps.security_note: Review exports for sensitive data before storing

steps.action: Upload to the evidence repository with a descriptive filename and evidence ID reference
Configure scheduled exports from cloud provider's native reporting tools (activity logs, compliance reports)
Set up automated delivery to evidence repository via email or storage integration
Create compliance dashboard in cloud provider's native console for visual evidence
Create service account or API credentials with read-only access to audit and compliance APIs
Implement automated daily collection scripts calling provider APIs for logs, configuration state, and compliance data
Configure alerting for collection failures and data volume anomalies
Validate collected evidence against expected coverage — all accounts, all regions, all resource types
Deploy cloud-native compliance framework (e.g., Security Hub, Defender for Cloud, Security Command Center)
Build evidence dashboard tracking compliance scores, remediation velocity, and coverage metrics over time
Implement automated evidence package generation with trend analysis and KPI reporting
Sign in to your identity provider's admin console with a read-only admin role
steps.security_note: Use the minimum privilege role that can access reports

steps.action: Navigate to Reports or Logs section and select authentication/sign-in logs
Set the date range to cover the evidence collection period with no gaps from the last collection
steps.audit_note: Check your provider's log retention period to avoid data loss

steps.action: Export the filtered log data as CSV and upload to the evidence repository
Configure scheduled reports for authentication events, access changes, and policy violations
Set up automated report delivery to evidence collection mailbox or shared storage
Create identity dashboard showing authentication patterns and policy compliance
Create API credentials with read-only access to identity provider's log and audit APIs
Automate daily extraction of authentication events, access reviews, and privilege changes
Implement volume and completeness validation for collected identity evidence
Configure alerting for collection failures and suspicious identity events
Deploy identity governance automation — scheduled access reviews, privilege attestation, dormant account detection
Build identity evidence dashboard: MFA adoption, access review completion rates, privilege escalation trends
Generate automated monthly identity governance evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your ticketing system and navigate to the relevant project or queue
Create a filter or search query for the evidence period (e.g., change requests created in the last 7 days)
steps.audit_note: Save your filter for repeatable future collections

steps.action: Export the filtered results as CSV including ticket ID, status, approvals, and dates
Upload the export to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
Create saved filters for compliance-relevant ticket types (changes, incidents, requests)
Configure scheduled export of filtered results as CSV or PDF reports
Set up automated delivery to evidence repository
Create API integration to query ticketing system for change, incident, and problem records
Automate daily extraction with data validation — verify required fields are populated
Implement webhook integration for real-time evidence capture on ticket closure
Build ITSM metrics dashboard: change success rate, MTTR, SLA compliance, approval cycle time
Implement automated compliance scoring for ITSM processes
Generate automated monthly ITSM evidence package with KPI trends
Sign in to your SIEM or log management platform
Create a search query for the evidence type you need (e.g., authentication events, configuration changes)
steps.audit_note: Document your search query for reproducibility and audit trail

steps.action: Set the time range to the evidence collection period and execute the search
Export results as CSV or JSON and upload to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Check that exported logs do not contain passwords or tokens in cleartext

steps.action: Create saved searches for evidence-relevant log queries and schedule weekly execution
Configure automated export of search results to evidence repository
Set up logging dashboard for visual evidence of monitoring activity
Create API integration for programmatic log search and export
Implement automated collection completeness monitoring — track data sources and ingestion health
Configure alerting for log collection failures and volume anomalies
Deploy security analytics framework for structured compliance evidence
Build evidence quality dashboard: completeness, timeliness, accuracy metrics
Generate automated monthly log monitoring evidence package with trend analysis
Sign in to your security tool's management console
Navigate to the Reports or Detections section relevant to your evidence need
Set date range filters and any relevant severity or category filters
steps.audit_note: Include the tool version and scan coverage in your evidence notes

steps.action: Export the report as PDF or CSV and upload to the evidence repository
Configure scheduled reports for security findings, coverage metrics, and policy compliance
Set up automated report delivery to evidence repository
Create security posture dashboard as visual evidence
Create API credentials for automated access to security tool's detection and coverage APIs
Implement daily automated collection of findings, coverage data, and policy compliance state
Configure SIEM integration for real-time security event streaming
Set up alerting for collection failures and coverage gaps
Build security evidence dashboard: MTTR, coverage %, detection fidelity, policy compliance trends
Implement automated evidence quality validation and completeness scoring
Generate automated monthly security evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your code repository platform and navigate to organization/project settings
Access the audit log or activity stream for the organization
steps.permissions_required: Organization admin or audit log viewer

steps.action: Filter events by date range and event type (e.g., repository changes, access changes, branch protection)
steps.audit_note: Note the log retention period for your plan tier

steps.action: Export filtered events and upload to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference
Configure scheduled exports of audit logs, access reviews, and security scanning results
Set up automated delivery of branch protection compliance reports
Create development security dashboard as visual evidence
Create API integration for automated audit log and security scanning result collection
Automate branch protection compliance verification across all repositories
Configure CI/CD pipeline evidence capture — build logs, security scan results, deployment records
Build DevSecOps evidence dashboard: code review coverage, security alert MTTR, policy compliance rate
Implement automated compliance gating in CI/CD as continuous evidence
Generate automated monthly DevSecOps evidence package with security metrics and trends
Access your document management system and locate the relevant policy or procedure documents
Verify the document version, approval date, and next review date
steps.audit_note: Document version history provides evidence of regular review cycles

steps.action: Export the document as PDF (including metadata) and upload to the evidence repository
If available, export the version history or approval workflow as additional evidence
Configure scheduled reports for document access logs, sharing changes, and DLP policy events
Set up automated delivery of audit reports to evidence repository
Create document governance dashboard as visual evidence
Create API integration for automated access log and sharing audit collection
Automate DLP policy compliance evidence extraction
Configure alerting for sensitive document access anomalies
Build document governance dashboard: classification coverage, DLP policy compliance, access review completion
Implement automated evidence quality scoring for document management controls
Generate automated monthly document governance evidence package with trend analysis
Sign in to your MDM platform's admin console
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated read-only account for evidence collection, not a full device administrator login

steps.action: Navigate to the device inventory or compliance section and review enrolled device counts against your asset register
steps.audit_note: Comparing MDM enrolment against the asset register evidences inventory completeness

steps.action: Filter devices by compliance status and encryption state (e.g., non-compliant, encryption disabled)
Export the device compliance and encryption status report as CSV
steps.security_note: Device exports contain hostnames and user identifiers — handle as internal-only data

steps.action: Upload the export to the evidence repository with a descriptive filename and evidence ID reference
Configure scheduled reports in your MDM platform for device compliance, encryption status, and enrolment coverage
Set up automated report delivery to the evidence collection mailbox or shared storage
Create a device compliance dashboard in the admin console as visual evidence of ongoing monitoring
Create API credentials with read-only access to your MDM platform's device and compliance APIs
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager; never embed them in scripts

steps.action: Implement automated daily collection of device inventory, compliance state, and encryption status
Validate collected data against expected coverage — all device platforms and all managed user populations
steps.audit_note: Coverage validation evidences that no device population is silently excluded

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and unexpected drops in enrolled device counts
Integrate MDM compliance events into your SIEM or compliance platform for continuous device posture evidence
Build an endpoint evidence dashboard: enrolment coverage %, encryption compliance %, patch currency, and time-to-remediate non-compliant devices
Implement automated reconciliation between MDM inventory, asset register, and identity directory with gap alerting
Generate automated monthly endpoint compliance evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your vulnerability management platform's console
Navigate to the scan results or reports section and confirm recent scans completed across the expected asset scope
steps.audit_note: Record scan coverage (assets scanned vs total assets) — auditors look for coverage gaps, not just findings

steps.action: Filter findings by severity and remediation status for the evidence period
Export the vulnerability report as PDF or CSV, including scan dates, severities, and remediation ages
steps.security_note: Vulnerability reports are sensitive — restrict access in the evidence repository to authorised reviewers

steps.action: Upload to the evidence repository with a descriptive filename and evidence ID reference
Configure recurring scan schedules covering all in-scope assets and confirm they align with your policy's scan frequency
Set up scheduled reports for open findings by severity, remediation SLA compliance, and scan coverage
Configure automated report delivery to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Deliver reports to a restricted location — vulnerability data should not go to broad distribution lists

steps.action: Create API credentials with read-only access to your platform's findings and asset APIs
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager and rotate on a defined schedule

steps.action: Implement automated daily extraction of findings, remediation status, and scan coverage data
Calculate remediation SLA compliance automatically — time from detection to closure by severity band
steps.audit_note: Automated SLA calculation from raw finding timestamps is stronger evidence than manually compiled figures

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures, missed scans, and unexpected drops in asset coverage
Integrate vulnerability findings into your ticketing system so every finding above the SLA threshold has a tracked remediation record
Build a vulnerability evidence dashboard: scan coverage %, mean time to remediate by severity, SLA compliance rate, and ageing backlog trends
Implement automated reconciliation between scanner asset inventory and the asset register with gap alerting
Generate automated monthly vulnerability management evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your email security gateway or mail platform's admin console
steps.permissions_required: Read-only administrator or security reader role

steps.action: Navigate to the policy configuration section and capture the current anti-phishing, anti-malware, and spam filtering policies
steps.audit_note: Screenshot or export policy settings with visible timestamps to evidence the configuration in force

steps.action: Export quarantine or threat detection reports for the evidence period showing blocked and quarantined messages
Verify DMARC, SPF, and DKIM records for your sending domains using your DNS provider or a public checker, and capture the results
steps.audit_note: Include the DMARC policy mode (none/quarantine/reject) — auditors distinguish monitoring-only from enforcing configurations

steps.action: Upload all exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
Configure scheduled threat and quarantine reports in your email security platform
Enable DMARC aggregate report collection to a monitored mailbox or reporting service
steps.audit_note: DMARC aggregate reports provide independent third-party evidence of email authentication enforcement

steps.action: Set up automated report delivery to the evidence repository
Create API credentials with read-only access to your platform's threat, quarantine, and policy APIs
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager; scope them to reporting endpoints only

steps.action: Implement automated daily collection of threat detections, quarantine actions, and policy configuration state
Automate DNS record checks for SPF, DKIM, and DMARC across all sending domains, alerting on drift
steps.audit_note: Scheduled DNS checks evidence continuous enforcement, not just point-in-time configuration

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and unexpected changes to filtering policies
Stream email security events into your SIEM for continuous threat detection evidence
Build an email security dashboard: block/quarantine rates, DMARC pass rates by domain, policy drift events, and user-reported phishing volumes
Implement automated policy drift detection comparing live configuration against the approved baseline
Generate automated monthly email security evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your security awareness training platform's admin console
Navigate to the training reports section and export completion status for all assigned campaigns
steps.audit_note: Include the assigned population count, not just completions — auditors assess completion rates against the full target audience

steps.action: Export phishing simulation results for the evidence period, including click rates and report rates
steps.security_note: Simulation results identify individual employees — handle as confidential HR-adjacent data

steps.action: Export evidence of follow-up actions for non-completers or repeat clickers (reminders, escalations, remedial training)
Upload exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
Configure scheduled reports for training completion, overdue assignments, and phishing simulation outcomes
Enable automated reminder and escalation workflows for overdue training, retaining the notification logs as evidence
Set up automated report delivery to the evidence repository
Create API credentials with read-only access to your platform's training and phishing simulation APIs
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager; never embed them in scripts

steps.action: Implement automated extraction of completion status, simulation results, and campaign coverage
Reconcile the training audience against your HR system or identity directory to confirm all active staff are enrolled
steps.audit_note: Audience reconciliation evidences that new joiners are captured and leavers excluded from completion metrics

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and completion rates falling below policy thresholds
Integrate the awareness platform with your HR system so enrolment, joiner training, and leaver removal are fully automated
Build an awareness evidence dashboard: completion rate by department, phishing click and report rate trends, and repeat-clicker remediation status
Implement automated escalation workflows with tracked outcomes for non-completion and repeat simulation failures
Generate automated quarterly awareness programme evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your password manager's admin console
steps.security_note: Admin access to a password manager is highly sensitive — ensure MFA is enforced on the account used

steps.action: Navigate to the policies section and capture the current vault access policies (MFA enforcement, master password requirements, session settings)
steps.audit_note: Screenshot or export policy settings with visible timestamps to evidence the configuration in force

steps.action: Export the vault or collection sharing report showing which vaults are shared with which users and groups
steps.security_note: Export metadata only — never export vault contents or credentials as compliance evidence

steps.action: Export the admin audit log for the evidence period covering user provisioning, permission changes, and policy changes
Upload exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
Configure scheduled reports for vault access, sharing changes, and security posture (weak or reused password counts where available)
Set up automated delivery of admin audit log exports to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Deliver to a restricted location — audit logs reveal who can access which credential stores

steps.action: Schedule a recurring access review of shared vaults, retaining the review sign-off as evidence
Create API credentials with read-only access to your password manager's audit and reporting APIs
steps.security_note: Scope credentials to audit/reporting endpoints only — never grant vault item read access to the collection integration

steps.action: Implement automated extraction of admin audit events, membership changes, and sharing configuration
Reconcile password manager users against your identity directory to detect leavers with residual access
steps.audit_note: Leaver reconciliation is a common audit finding — automated evidence of the check is stronger than periodic manual review

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and high-risk events such as vault exports or emergency access use
Stream admin audit events into your SIEM for continuous monitoring of credential store access
Integrate provisioning with your identity provider (SCIM/SSO) so joiner/leaver access is automated and evidenced
Build a credential governance dashboard: MFA adoption, sharing review completion, security score trends, and high-risk event counts
Generate automated quarterly credential governance evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your chat or meeting platform's admin console
Navigate to the retention settings and capture the current message and file retention policies
steps.audit_note: Screenshot or export retention settings with visible timestamps — retention configuration is point-in-time evidence

steps.action: Capture the external access and guest sharing policies (which external domains or guests are permitted, and under what restrictions)
Export the admin audit log for the evidence period covering policy changes, external sharing events, and admin actions
steps.security_note: Audit logs may contain user names and channel names — handle as internal-only data

steps.action: Upload exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
Configure scheduled exports of admin audit logs and external access reports where the platform supports them
Schedule a recurring review of guest accounts and external shared channels, retaining the review sign-off as evidence
Set up automated report delivery to the evidence repository
Create API credentials with read-only access to your platform's audit log and admin APIs
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager; scope them to audit endpoints, not message content

steps.action: Implement automated extraction of admin audit events, guest account inventory, and external access configuration
Automate detection of retention policy or external access policy changes against the approved baseline
steps.audit_note: Baseline drift detection evidences continuous policy enforcement rather than point-in-time screenshots

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and high-risk changes such as retention reduction or new external domains
Stream admin audit events into your SIEM for continuous monitoring of collaboration governance
Build a collaboration governance dashboard: guest account counts and age, external channel inventory, retention compliance, and policy drift events
Implement automated guest access lifecycle management — expiry, review, and removal with evidenced outcomes
Generate automated quarterly collaboration governance evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
Sign in to your HR system with a role limited to reporting
steps.permissions_required: HR reporting or read-only administrator role
steps.security_note: HR data is highly sensitive personal data — use the minimum access needed and follow your data protection policy

steps.action: Run the joiners, movers, and leavers report for the evidence period, including start dates, role changes, and termination dates
steps.audit_note: Leaver dates are cross-checked against access removal evidence — ensure the date fields are included in the export

steps.action: Export evidence of onboarding and offboarding checklist completion for a sample of the period's joiners and leavers
Export the list of users with administrative access to the HR system itself
steps.audit_note: Access to HR data is itself an audited control — evidence who can view and change employee records

steps.action: Upload exports to the evidence repository with descriptive filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Minimise personal data in evidence — redact fields not needed to demonstrate the control

steps.action: Configure scheduled joiner/mover/leaver reports delivered to the teams responsible for access provisioning and deprovisioning
Set up automated delivery of lifecycle reports to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Deliver to a restricted location — JML reports contain personal data

steps.action: Schedule a recurring review of HR system access rights, retaining the review sign-off as evidence
Create API credentials with read-only access to your HR system's employee lifecycle data, scoped to the minimum fields required
steps.security_note: Store credentials in a secrets manager; limit the integration to lifecycle fields, not salary or personal detail

steps.action: Implement automated daily extraction of joiner, mover, and leaver events
Reconcile leaver events against identity provider account status to evidence timely access removal
steps.audit_note: Automated leaver-to-deprovisioning reconciliation with timestamps is the strongest form of JML evidence

steps.action: Configure alerting for collection failures and leavers whose accounts remain active beyond the policy deadline

L4.title: HR System Managed Lifecycle Evidence Pipeline

steps.action: Integrate the HR system as the authoritative source for identity lifecycle automation (HR-driven provisioning and deprovisioning)
Build a lifecycle evidence dashboard: time-to-provision for joiners, time-to-deprovision for leavers, mover access recertification completion, and exception counts
Implement automated exception workflows for overdue deprovisioning with tracked remediation records
Generate automated quarterly employee lifecycle evidence package with KPIs and trend analysis
